description: §112(a)/(b)/(f) US conformance and equivalents in EP (Art 83 / 84 / 123(2)), CN (Art 26), JP (Art 36). Jurisdictional conformity is scored only for use cases that explicitly target the corresponding jurisdiction.
sub_criteria: §112(a) enablement — POSITA can make and use
§112(b) definiteness — clear claim terms, no ambiguity
§112(f) means-plus-function handling — disclosed structure for any functional language
EP Art 83 sufficiency + Art 84 clarity / conciseness
EP Art 123(2) added-matter screening (no new matter post-filing)

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: Specification fully enables a POSITA to make and use every claimed embodiment; written description support is explicit for every limitation in every claim; §112(b) terms are unambiguous; §112(f) means-plus-function language, where used, is bounded by clearly disclosed structure. Where the use case targets foreign jurisdictions, EP Art 83/84/123(2), CN Art 26.3/.4, and JP Art 36 requirements are satisfied without preparatory amendment.

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: Specification is sound on §112(a) enablement and §112(b) definiteness in the US. Minor drafting tightening would close marginal definiteness gaps (e.g., a relative term lacking a reference, a functional limitation with thin structural support). For multi-jurisdiction inventions, EP / CN / JP requirements are met with one or two non-blocking observations.

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: Filable specification with §112(a) enablement gaps requiring more than one editing pass to resolve. Some claim limitations have thin or implicit written description support that would survive examination but not a rigorous IPR. Jurisdictional conformance is uneven across EP / CN / JP.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: Material §112(a) enablement or §112(b) definiteness failures: written description does not support multiple claim limitations; means-plus-function language lacks disclosed structure; relative terms without reference appear in the claims. Would face strong §112 rejection on substantive examination.

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: Specification is non-enabling as a whole, lacks definite claim language across multiple claims, or contains added matter that would prompt EP Art 123(2) rejection on filing in any jurisdiction. The draft would be unfilable without substantial rewriting of the disclosure.

track_a_signals: BERTScore against ground-truth specification on per-section basis
Coverage of disclosed embodiments and §112(a) supporting language
track_b_protocol: Reviewer scores 1-5 against §112 + jurisdictional sub-criteria. For multi-jurisdiction inventions, separate scores per jurisdiction are recorded then averaged with weights 0.5 US, 0.2 EP, 0.15 CN, 0.15 JP unless otherwise specified by the test case.
